revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鰐河神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 香川県木田郡三木町下高岡1843 |位置 = |祭神 = 豊玉姫命 |社格 = 式内社 郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]にある[[神社]]。[[讃岐国]][[三木郡]]の[[式内社]]和尓賀波神社の論社のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。
同じく和尓賀波神社の論社の[[白山神社 (三木町)|白山神社]]は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。
==祭神== *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] *[[応神天皇]]
==概略== 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、豊玉姫神が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って讃岐国[[山田郡 (讃岐国)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[サメ|鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという。
[[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。
[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され郷社となる。
== その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子|獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の[[聖観音|脱乾漆聖観音坐像]]（国指定重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。
==交通== *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。
{{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:三木町]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 鰐河神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 香川県木田郡三木町下高岡1843 |位置 = |祭神 = 豊玉姫命 |社格 = 式内社 郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}

== その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子|獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。
==交通== *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。

== その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子|獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。

==概略== 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、[[豊玉姫命]]が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って[[讃岐国]][[山田郡 (香川県)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。
また[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。
[[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。
[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され[[郷社]]となる。
== その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。
==交通== *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称= 鰐河神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|鰐河神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]大字下高岡1843番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[豊玉姫命]]・[[応神天皇]] |社格= [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[流造]] |別名= 四条大宮 |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=鰐河神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]にある[[神社]]。[[讃岐国]][[三木郡]]高岡郷の[[式内社]]和爾賀波神社の[[論社]]のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の[[白山神社 (三木町)|白山神社]]は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 == 祭神 == *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] *[[応神天皇]] == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、[[豊玉姫命]]が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って[[讃岐国]][[山田郡 (香川県)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 [[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され[[郷社]]となる。 == その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。 == 交通 == *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Wanikawa Shrine'''|鰐河神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sanuki Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 鰐河神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|鰐河神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]大字下高岡1843番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[豊玉姫命]]・[[応神天皇]] |社格= [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[流造]] |別名= 四条大宮 |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=鰐河神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]にある[[神社]]。[[讃岐国]][[三木郡]]高岡郷の[[式内社]]和爾賀波神社の[[論社]]のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の[[白山神社 (三木町)|白山神社]]は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 == 祭神 == *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] *[[応神天皇]] == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、[[豊玉姫命]]が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って[[讃岐国]][[山田郡 (香川県)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 [[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され[[郷社]]となる。 == その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。 == 交通 == *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称= 鰐河神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|鰐河神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]大字下高岡1843番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[豊玉姫命]]・[[応神天皇]] |社格= [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[流造]] |別名= 四条大宮 |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=鰐河神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]にある[[神社]]。[[讃岐国]][[三木郡]]高岡郷の[[式内社]]和爾賀波神社の[[論社]]のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の[[白山神社 (三木町)|白山神社]]は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 == 祭神 == *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] *[[応神天皇]] == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、[[豊玉姫命]]が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って[[讃岐国]][[山田郡 (香川県)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 [[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され[[郷社]]となる。 == その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。 == 交通 == *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称= 鰐河神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|鰐河神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]大字下高岡1843番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[豊玉姫命]]・[[応神天皇]] |社格= [[式内小社]]・旧[[郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[流造]] |別名= 四条大宮 |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=鰐河神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、[[香川県]][[木田郡]][[三木町]]にある[[神社]]。[[讃岐国]][[三木郡]]高岡郷の[[式内社]]和爾賀波神社の[[論社]]のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の[[白山神社 (三木町)|白山神社]]は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 == 祭神 == *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]] *[[応神天皇]] == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、[[豊玉姫命]]が[[ウミガメ科|亀]]に乗って[[讃岐国]][[山田郡 (香川県)|山田郡]]潟元（[[屋島]]付近）に上陸し、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鵜葺草葺不合尊]]をこの地で産む。その後[[鰐]]に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また[[行基]]がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 [[延喜]]7年（[[907年]]）に[[八幡神]]を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また[[神仏習合]]により[[別当寺]]として応神寺が創建されている。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）の[[神仏分離]]で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（[[1872年]]）鰐河神社に改称され[[郷社]]となる。 == その他 == *鰐河神社の[[獅子舞]]の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。[[獅子舞]]は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。[[三木町獅子舞フェスタ]]などで見ることが可能。 *[[さぬき市]]の[[願興寺 (さぬき市)|願興寺]]の脱活乾漆[[聖観音]]坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の[[新川 (香川県)|新川]]と言われている。尚、近くにある[[和爾賀波神社]]（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は[[鴨部川]]のことという。 == 交通 == *[[高松琴平電気鉄道]][[高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|長尾線]][[白山駅 (香川県)|白山駅]]より徒歩20分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= 四条大宮 |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=鰐河神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}高岡郷の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 == 祭神 == *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} == 概略 == 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、{{jalink|豊玉姫命}}が{{jalink|ウミガメ科|Q30143|亀}}に乗って{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|山田郡 (香川県)|Q11046466|山田郡}}潟元（{{jalink|屋島|Q11465681}}付近）に上陸し、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|鵜葺草葺不合尊}}をこの地で産む。その後{{jalink|鰐|Q11674313}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 {{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}7年（{{jalink|907年|Q24163}}）に{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}として応神寺が創建されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）鰐河神社に改称され{{jalink|郷社}}となる。 == その他 == *鰐河神社の{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。{{jalink|三木町獅子舞フェスタ}}などで見ることが可能。 *{{jalink|さぬき市|Q857202}}の{{jalink|願興寺 (さぬき市)||願興寺}}の脱活乾漆{{jalink|聖観音|Q124684411}}坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}と言われている。尚、近くにある{{jalink|和爾賀波神社|Q11417869}}（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという。 == 交通 == *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|白山駅 (香川県)|Q11579619|白山駅}}より徒歩20分。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

'''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}高岡郷の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、{{jalink|豊玉姫命}}が{{jalink|ウミガメ科|Q30143|亀}}に乗って{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|山田郡 (香川県)|Q11046466|山田郡}}潟元（{{jalink|屋島|Q11465681}}付近）に上陸し、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|鵜葺草葺不合尊}}をこの地で産む。その後{{jalink|鰐|Q11674313}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 {{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}7年（{{jalink|907年|Q24163}}）に{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}として応神寺が創建されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）鰐河神社に改称され{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *鰐河神社の{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。{{jalink|三木町獅子舞フェスタ}}などで見ることが可能。 *{{jalink|さぬき市|Q857202}}の{{jalink|願興寺 (さぬき市)||願興寺}}の脱活乾漆{{jalink|聖観音|Q124684411}}坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}と言われている。尚、近くにある{{jalink|和爾賀波神社|Q11417869}}（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|白山駅 (香川県)|Q11579619|白山駅}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

創建時期は不明。社伝によると、{{jalink|豊玉姫命}}が{{jalink|ウミガメ科|Q30143|亀}}に乗って{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|山田郡 (香川県)|Q11046466|山田郡}}潟元（{{jalink|屋島|Q11465681}}付近）に上陸し、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|鵜葺草葺不合尊}}をこの地で産む。その後{{jalink|鰐|Q11674313}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 {{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}7年（{{jalink|907年|Q24163}}）に{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}として応神寺が創建されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）鰐河神社に改称され{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *鰐河神社の{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。{{jalink|三木町獅子舞フェスタ}}などで見ることが可能。 *{{jalink|さぬき市|Q857202}}の{{jalink|願興寺 (さぬき市)||願興寺}}の脱活乾漆{{jalink|聖観音|Q124684411}}坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}と言われている。尚、近くにある{{jalink|和爾賀波神社|Q11417869}}（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|白山駅 (香川県)|Q11579619|白山駅}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

(香川県)|Q11046466|山田郡}}潟元（{{jalink|屋島|Q11465681}}付近）に上陸し、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|鵜葺草葺不合尊}}をこの地で産む。その後{{jalink|鰐|Q11674313}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 {{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}7年（{{jalink|907年|Q24163}}）に{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}として応神寺が創建されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）鰐河神社に改称され{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *鰐河神社の{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。{{jalink|三木町獅子舞フェスタ}}などで見ることが可能。 *{{jalink|さぬき市|Q857202}}の{{jalink|願興寺 (さぬき市)||願興寺}}の脱活乾漆{{jalink|聖観音|Q124684411}}坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}と言われている。尚、近くにある{{jalink|和爾賀波神社|Q11417869}}（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|白山駅 (香川県)|Q11579619|白山駅}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

'''鰐河神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}高岡郷の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。さぬき十五社の一つ。 同じく和爾賀波神社の論社の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}は、鰐河神社の境外末社という説がある。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉姫命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 概略 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、{{jalink|豊玉姫命}}が{{jalink|ウミガメ科|Q30143|亀}}に乗って{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|山田郡 (香川県)|Q11046466|山田郡}}潟元（{{jalink|屋島|Q11465681}}付近）に上陸し、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|鵜葺草葺不合尊}}をこの地で産む。その後{{jalink|鰐|Q11674313}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。 また{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}がこの地に創建したという伝承もある。 {{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}7年（{{jalink|907年|Q24163}}）に{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}を配祀し、高岡八幡とも称していた。また{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}として応神寺が創建されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）の{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}で応神寺は廃止される。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）鰐河神社に改称され{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *鰐河神社の{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}の獅子頭は、高さ63cm・幅90cm・長さ100cm。獅子舞油単を合わせると全長約13mになる。{{jalink|獅子舞|Q713866}}は50人がかりで動かすという巨大なものであり、三木町指定文化財である。{{jalink|三木町獅子舞フェスタ}}などで見ることが可能。 *{{jalink|さぬき市|Q857202}}の{{jalink|願興寺 (さぬき市)||願興寺}}の脱活乾漆{{jalink|聖観音|Q124684411}}坐像（国の重要文化財）は、鰐河神社の別当寺の応神寺の本尊という言い伝えがある。 *豊玉姫神が鰐に乗って遡った川は、後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}と言われている。尚、近くにある{{jalink|和爾賀波神社|Q11417869}}（和尓賀波神社の論社）によると鰐川は{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|白山駅 (香川県)|Q11579619|白山駅}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa '香川県木田郡三木町大字下高岡1843番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '香川県木田郡三木町井上', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鰐河神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674320}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}{{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Takaoka-go, {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} rode a {{jalink|sea turtle|Q30143|turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{jalink|Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|Yamada District}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}} (near {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{jalink|wani|Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} 7 ({{jalink|907|Q24163}}), the {{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{jalink|shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{jalink|Shō-Kannon|Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{jalink|Sanuki|Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|Hakusan Station}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}{{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Takaoka-go, {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} rode a {{jalink|sea turtle|Q30143|turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{jalink|Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|Yamada District}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}} (near {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{jalink|wani|Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} 7 ({{jalink|907|Q24163}}), the {{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{jalink|shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{jalink|Shō-Kannon|Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{jalink|Sanuki|Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|Hakusan Station}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

'香川県木田郡三木町大字下高岡1843番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '香川県木田郡三木町井上', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鰐河神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674320}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}{{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of Takaoka-go, {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} rode a {{jalink|sea turtle|Q30143|turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{jalink|Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|Yamada District}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}} (near {{jalink|Yashima|Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{jalink|wani|Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{jalink|Engi|Q1374325}} 7 ({{jalink|907|Q24163}}), the {{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{jalink|shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{jalink|lion dance|Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{jalink|Shō-Kannon|Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{jalink|Sanuki|Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|Hakusan Station}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135041387|lt=Q135041387|WD=Q135041387}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '香川県木田郡三木町大字下高岡1843番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '香川県木田郡三木町井上', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鰐河神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674320}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill||lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill||lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'香川県木田郡三木町大字下高岡1843番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '香川県木田郡三木町井上', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鰐河神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11674320}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle|1=Cheloniidae}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313|1=wani}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163|1=907}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle|1=Cheloniidae}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313|1=wani}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163|1=907}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Shimotakaoka.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}} Shimotakaoka 1843 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}・{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Shijō Ōmiya |札所= |例祭= first Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki, Kagawa|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle|1=Cheloniidae}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313|1=wani}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163|1=907}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

It is one of the candidate shrines for Wanigawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of Takaoka-go, {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. It is one of the “Sanuki Fifteen Shrines.” There is a theory that {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}}, also a candidate shrine for Wanigawa Shrine, is an outside branch shrine of Wanikawa Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Outline === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle|1=Cheloniidae}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313|1=wani}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163|1=907}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

According to shrine tradition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} rode a {{ill|lt=sea turtle|Q30143|WD=Q30143|lt=turtle|1=Cheloniidae}} ashore at Kata-moto in {{ill|lt=Yamada District (Kagawa)|Q11046466|WD=Q11046466|lt=Yamada District|1=Yamada district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}} (near {{ill|lt=Yashima|Q11465681|WD=Q11465681|1=Yashima}}), gave birth to {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} there, then rode a {{ill|lt=wani|Q11674313|WD=Q11674313|1=wani}} upstream and landed here. There is also a tradition that {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} founded it here. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=907|Q24163|WD=Q24163|1=907}}), the {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined as an associated deity, and it was also called Takaoka Hachiman. Through {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, Ōjin-ji was also founded as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Ōjin-ji was abolished in the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was renamed Wanikawa Shrine and became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === * The lion head for Wanikawa Shrine’s {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is 63 cm high, 90 cm wide, and 100 cm long; with the dance cloth, total length is about 13 m. This giant lion dance is moved by about 50 people and is a designated cultural property of Miki Town. * The seated dry-lacquer {{ill|lt=Shō-Kannon|Q124684411|WD=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} statue at Gankō-ji in {{ill|lt=Sanuki|Q857202|WD=Q857202|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}} is said in tradition to have been the principal image of Ōjin-ji, the bettō temple of Wanikawa Shrine. * The river that Toyotama-hime rode upstream on a wani later became Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. According to nearby {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11417869|WD=Q11417869|1=Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}} (another candidate shrine), Wani River refers to {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}. === Access === * 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hakusan Station (Kagawa)|Q11579619|WD=Q11579619|lt=Hakusan Station|1=Shirayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]
